key_insight: Instead of tempering an entire supramolecular system at once, the paper builds the polymer sequentially by adding one monomer at a time and using REST to optimize each new binding step.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the replica-count and traversal burden of global REST, making atomistic modeling of supramolecular polymer growth more computationally practical.
method: Add monomers one by one under an explicit-solvent model.
Apply REST only to the newly added monomers to sample binding positions.
Use an energy-based scoring function to select the best structure at each growth step.
evidence_signal: In a model supramolecular polymer test, the sequential REST approach reproduced characteristic H-bonding patterns while improving replica traversal efficiency and lowering computational cost.
watch_item: The approach assumes one-dimensional polymerization, so it may not generalize well to systems with branching, competing morphologies, or non-sequential assembly pathways.
paper_title: Sequential replica exchange with solute tempering for atomistic modeling of supramolecular polymer structures
